texto: It wasn't like the World Trade Center, something vile and astonishing within our own borders, happening to people who'd saved coins of the same currency in their piggybanks when they were children. I knew intellectually that shouldn't make a difference, but it seemed to.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Michael Marshall: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “It wasn't like the World Trade Center, something vile and astonishing within our own borders, hap…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, empathy, nationalism pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de nationalism.

texto: Just because we are capable of art didn't what lay in front of him could be dismissed as aberration, that we could take what we admired and fence that off as human, dismissing the rest as monstrous. The same hands committed both. Brains don't undermine the savagery. They made us better at it.
explicacao: Citação de Michael Marshall sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Just because we are capable of art didn't what lay in front of him could be dismissed as aberrati…” articula reflexao, human-nature, could em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como dia-chuvoso e reflexao.

texto: The food chain has victims at both ends: even rapists and murderers need someone to look down on, and kid killers will do nicely.
explicacao: Citação de Michael Marshall sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “The food chain has victims at both ends: even rapists and murderers need someone to look down on,…” articula reflexao, justice, prison em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e prison.

texto: The body protects itself, and the same happens in the mind. It occurs sluggishly and imperfectly, a bad job done by indifferent craftsmen, but within minutes an accretion of defence mechanisms starts to form around the trauma, blunting its edges, eventually sealing it away inside scar tissue. Like a sliver of glass buried deep in a cut, the event will never go away, and often a movement will cause it to nudge a nerve ending and burn like fire for a while. However much it hurts when that happens, the last thing you want.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Michael Marshall: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “The body protects itself, and the same happens in the mind. It occurs sluggishly and imperfectly,…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, healing, pain pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e pain.

It occurs sluggishly and imperfectly, a bad job done by indifferent craftsmen, but within minutes an accretion of defence mechanisms starts to form around the trauma, blunting its edges, eventually sealing it away inside scar tissue. Like a sliver of glass buried deep in a cut, the event will never go away, and often a movement will cause it to nudge a nerve ending and burn like fire for a while. However much it hurts when that happens, the last thing you want.
